steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Start with $x^2+6x+4$. Half of $6$ is $3$, and $3^2=9$, so rewrite the expression by adding and subtracting $9$:
$$x^2+6x+4=x^2+6x+9+4-9$$

steps.title: Convert to vertex form
steps.content:
The first three terms make a perfect square:
$$x^2+6x+9=(x+3)^2$$
The constants combine to $4-9=-5$, so:
$$x^2+6x+4=(x+3)^2-5$$

steps.title: Desmos overlap check
steps.content: Graph the original expression and choice B. The two graphs overlap exactly, confirming the equivalent form.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The matching expression is $(x+3)^2-5$, which is choice B. So B is correct.
